label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which statement is true based on the passage. The question is about the writer's memory of locking the front door. Let me read the relevant part again. The passage says: "One morning last week, for example, I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." So the writer found the door unlocked, but they remember locking it. That means the writer thought they locked it, but actually didn't. So they forgot to lock it. Let me look at the options. Option A says the writer forgot to lock the door. That seems right because they found it unlocked but remembered locking it. So their memory tricked them into thinking they did it when they didn't. Option B says the writer forgot having locked the door. Wait, that's different. If they forgot having locked it, that would mean they didn't remember that they had locked it. But the passage says they "clearly remember locking it." So B is not correct. Option C says the writer remembered to lock the door. Remembering to do something is different. Here, they did remember doing it, but they didn't actually do it. So C is not correct because they didn't actually lock it, even though they thought they did. Option D says the writer remembered unlocking the door. The passage says
